name: 25th July Cinema
description:
25th July Cinema Club is a collaborative hub for students passionate about the art of visual storytelling. Whether you’re into directing, screenwriting, acting, cinematography, or editing - this is where ideas turn into motion pictures. No prior experience needed - just your passion and imagination.


**Location:** 27 Block 146 Room, Dormitory


## Activities
- Create original short films and music videos
- Organize major events like Lights On! Filmathon and premiere nights
- Host workshops, feedback sessions, and production challenges
- Conduct creative meetups, movie quizzes, and guest lectures


## Achievements
- Named Art & Recreation Club of the Year 2025
- Hosted Lights On Filmathon with 150+ student filmmakers in a 24-hour challenge
- Organized 4+ movie quizzes and 2 guest lectures, including one with Aya Shalkar
- Created 2 live music videos and co-hosted a concert with Kunzharyq


## Entry Requirements
- Be open and creative
- Have a passion for filmmaking or films
- No prior experience needed - just enthusiasm
- Willingness to be involved, grow, and be part of a lasting, supportive community

name: Accounting and Auditing Club
description: Accounting and Auditing Club (AAC) is a professional development club founded by former employees of the Big 4. Our mission is to help students gain the knowledge, skills, and experience needed to build successful careers in accounting, auditing, and beyond. We organize large-scale events with industry-level standards, including case championships, professional bootcamps with Big 4 companies, intellectual games, and more. Our members also take part in educational lectures and hands-on workshops led by mentors, allowing them to deepen their understanding of key financial topics. Beyond professional growth, AAC offers a welcoming and supportive community where like-minded students passionate about finance can learn, connect, and grow together.

name: ACM@NU
description:
ACM@NU -is the Nazarbayev University branch of the Association for Computing Machinery, the world's largest educational and scientific computing society. Founded in 2017, it was the first ACM student chapter in Kazakhstan.
The club functions as the primary IT hub for students, focusing on networking, skill-building, and high-stakes competitions.

Since 2017, we’ve provided unique opportunities for networking, mentoring, and connecting over shared interests within the university and the wider community. The ACM-Women Student Chapter empowers women through projects and programs in computing.

name: Acoustic Night Community
description:
Acoustic Night has been a part of Nazarbayev University since 2014, bringing students together through the power of live acoustic music. We organize concerts both on and off campus, giving students the chance to share their musical talents and passion, not only as performers, but also as organizers, creatives, or simply music lovers. Whether you're performing or enjoying the music, our club creates a space where everyone can connect, relax, and celebrate the art of music. Everyone is welcome at AN, no experience needed, just a love for music and good vibes.


**Location:** 26 Block 122 Room, Dormitory


## Activities
- Acoustic concerts on and off campus
- Open mic nights
- Tutorial sessions for beginners


## Achievements
- Our charity concert raised 77,000 tenge


## Entry Requirements
- Everyone is welcome!
- If music moves you, you’re already one of us.

description: Official Adventure Club at Nazarbayev Universitys a student-run organization dedicated to outdoor activities, travel, and promoting an active lifestyle. Unlike academic or career-focused clubs like ACM, NUAC is purely recreational and focused on exploring Kazakhstan’s natural landscapes.

name: Aeromodelling Club
description:
Aeromodelling Club is a community for students passionate about aviation and aerospace technology. We focus on designing, building, and flying model aircraft, from fixed-wing planes to advanced RC models. Members have the opportunity to learn about aerodynamics, flight control systems, and modern engineering techniques through hands-on projects. The club also participates in competitions such as Teknofest, where students can showcase their skills and creativity. Whether you are an experienced builder or just curious about aircraft design, our club offers a space to innovate, collaborate, and bring ideas to life.


**Location:** 27 Block 144 room, Dormitory


## Activities
- Designing, building, and flying RC planes and drones
- AeroTalks


## Achievements
- Participated in Teknofest international UAV competition


## Entry Requirements
- Knowledge of aerodynamics
- 3D CAD modeling
- Flight controls
- Basic electronics proficiency

name: American Concrete Institute Student Chapter
description:
The American Concrete Institute (ACI) student chapter at our university is a civil engineering club dedicated to expanding students' practical knowledge in structural design, construction, and materials. Through workshops, site visits, and competitions, we provide opportunities to apply classroom concepts in real-world settings, connect with industry professionals, and build teamwork among future civil engineers.


**Location:** 27 Block 154 Room, Dormitory


## Activities
- Bridge and Tower construction tournaments
- Guest lectures with industry professionals
- Field trips
- Networking events with professors and students


## Achievements
- 2023-2024 Outstanding Student Chapter Award
- 2024 NU Student Awards Academic Club Nomination


## Entry Requirements
- Eager to learn about civil engineering
- Willing to participate in projects and activities
- Open to teamwork and collaboration
- Motivated to contribute to the growth of the civil engineering community

name: American Society of Chemical Engineers Student Chapter
description:
Official AIChE Student Chapter at Nazarbayev University (the American Institute of Chemical Engineers) is the professional home for students focused on the large-scale application of chemical and biological processes. While ACS focuses on the fundamental science and "lab-scale" chemistry, AIChE is dedicated to engineering, optimization, and industrial production.
Core Focus & Activities
Chem-E-Car Competition: Their most famous international activity. Students design and build a shoebox-sized car powered by a chemical reaction that must carry a specific load over a given distance and stop precisely using another reaction.
Industrial Site Visits: The chapter organizes trips to major industrial plants (such as oil refineries or pharmaceutical facilities) to give students a first-hand look at large-scale operations and safety protocols.
K-6 Outreach: Similar to the global AIChE mission, the NU chapter engages in "Stem-bassador" activities, teaching basic engineering principles to younger students through hands-on experiments.
Professional Networking: They host technical sessions and workshops on industry standards, including Process Safety (SAChE certification), which is highly valued in the global energy and manufacturing sectors.

name: Anime Club
description:
The following include the objectives of the club but not limited to:

Promote the anime & manga industry among the NU Community.

Provide NU students opportunities to start or continue their career path in the anime & manga industry.

Make it possible to present our own culture via the anime & manga industry.


**Location:** 27 Block 150 Room, Dormitory


## Activities
- Treasure Hunts
- Manga Coloring
- Movie Nights
- AnimeCon


## Achievements
- Contribution to socialization and networking of students
- 10% discount for NU students at GeekMarket with no expiration date
- Annual gathering of cosplayers from across Astana city to share experiences and have fun


## Entry Requirements
- Familiarity with anime and/or manga cultures
- Open for new experience

name: Association of Political Science Students
description:
The Official Association of Political Science Students (OAPSS) at Nazarbayev University is the premier student-led organization dedicated to fostering a robust academic and professional community for aspiring political scientists, diplomats, and policy analysts.
The association serves as a vital platform for bridging the gap between classroom theory and global political reality. Its mission is carried out through three primary pillars:
Intellectual Engagement: Hosting high-level debates, guest lectures, and seminars featuring prominent scholars, diplomats, and policymakers to deepen students' understanding of international and domestic affairs.
Professional Development: Providing networking opportunities, workshops, and career-oriented events that help students navigate the path from academia to the professional world.
Civic Awareness: Promoting critical thinking and political literacy within the university, encouraging students to engage analytically with current global trends and regional challenges.
In essence, OAPSS acts as a catalyst for leadership and research, empowering students to translate their academic insights into meaningful social and political contributions.

name: Association of Women in Mathematics
description:
The Association for Women in Mathematics (AWM) at NU promotes gender equity in STEM and beyond by organizing impactful events such as networking sessions with researchers and professionals, all-female Math Competitions, and mentorship programs for female students and schoolgirls. One of our key partners is TechnoWomen, with whom we collaborate to support and inspire future women leaders in science and technology. We provide a platform where female students can grow, lead, and connect through shared experiences in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics.


## Activities
- Women Empowerment Info Sessions
- AWM Math Combat
- STEMpowerment Talks
- Lunch with PhD


## Achievements
- Nominated for Breakthrough of the Year at NU Awards
- Organized the first-ever all-female Math Combat with 14 institutions
- Partnered with TechnoWomen for leadership events


## Entry Requirements
- Passion for equity
- Open to all majors and experience levels
- Eager to learn and contribute
- Supportive of a collaborative community

name: Astana Ball
description:
The Official Astana Ball at Nazarbayev University is one of the most prestigious and culturally significant student-led events at the university, blending classical elegance with a strong commitment to social responsibility.
The ball is primarily defined by its dual purpose. Organizing large events.


Charity and Community: Unlike typical gala events, the Astana Ball is fundamentally a charity evening. It serves as a platform for the NU community to support social causes, frequently partnering with organizations like the Baqytty Shańyraq foundation to assist families raising children with disabilities. +1 
Cultural Tradition: The highlight of the evening is the performance by the debutants—pairs of students who undergo months of rigorous training to master the classical waltz and polonaise. Each year, the ball adopts a unique theme (such as the 2024 Masquerade) to guide its aesthetic and performances. +1 
Student Leadership: The event is organized by the student club Astana Ball, showcasing high-level project management, event planning, and fundraising skills within the student body.  
In short, the Astana Ball is a "symbol of beauty and charity," where the university's elite artistic traditions meet a collective desire to make a tangible impact on society.

name: Ballroom Dance
description:
The Ballroom Dance Fam is a warm and inclusive community where anyone can discover the joy of ballroom dancing - absolutely free. Whether you’ve never danced before or already have experience, our club offers a supportive space to learn, practice, and have fun. No auditions, no prior skills, and no partner required - everyone is welcome!

The Ballroom Dance Club is a vibrant community for anyone passionate about dance - from complete beginners taking their first steps to seasoned dancers refining their skills. Our professional ballroom choreographers guide you through a wide variety of styles, including the Waltz, Tango, Foxtrot, Quickstep, Cha-Cha, and Rumba. Each session is designed to be engaging, approachable, and confidence-building, so you can enjoy dancing from your very first class.

You can see our fantastic performances at Astana Ball, NU Orchestra Grand Concert, NU Decides, Snowball, NU Awards, and many other events. Moreover, every year we organize our own concert, where every member who visited our club has the opportunity to shine on stage.

Whether you're an experienced dancer or a complete beginner, you'll find a friendly community and expert guidance here, because Ballroom is family! We offer regular practice sessions and workshops to help you shine on the dance floor. Discover the joy of ballroom dancing, make new friends, and create unforgettable memories with us!


**Location:** 26 Block 103 Room, Dormitory


## Activities
- Regular dance practices and technique workshops
- Choreography sessions for performances
- Social dancing events
- Annual end-of-year dance concert


## Achievements
- Performed at Prestigious Events – Featured at NU Decides, Astana Ball, NU Awards, and the NU Orchestra Grand Concert
- Organizes Themed Dance Concerts – Presented elegant performances across Waltz, Tango, Cha-Cha, and other ballroom styles
- Growth of New Dancers – Helped complete beginners become confident performers through free professional training


## Entry Requirements
- No dance experience required
- No partner needed
- No auditions or fees - all classes are free
- Open to all skill levels and backgrounds

- Charity collaborations

name: Belly Dance
description:
The Official Belly Dance Club at Nazarbayev University is a creative and athletic organization dedicated to the art of Middle Eastern dance. It provides a space where students can explore cultural expression through movement while improving their physical fitness and confidence.
The club focuses on several core pillars of the art form:
Technique and Artistry: Training members in the fundamental isolations, shimmies, and fluid movements characteristic of Raqs Sharqi (Oriental Dance). Workshops often cover different styles, from classical Egyptian to tribal fusion.
Physical Conditioning: Belly dance is an excellent low-impact workout. The club emphasizes core strength, flexibility, and posture, making it a popular choice for students looking for an alternative to traditional gym routines.
Performance and Choreography: Members work together to create intricate group choreographies for university-wide events, cultural festivals, and talent showcases, fostering teamwork and stage presence.
Cultural Appreciation: Beyond the dance itself, the club educates members on the history, music, and diverse cultural backgrounds of the Middle East, ensuring the art form is practiced with respect and understanding.

name: BioExplorer
description:
From thought-provoking discussions and guest speakers to biology-themed games and chill study sessions, we turn interest into impact. Whether you're obsessed with the logic behind life, fascinated by biology’s strangest exceptions, or just here for the vibes and cool facts, there’s a place for you. We’re here to make biology exciting, approachable, and a little chaotic in the best way.

name: Boxing Club
description:
The Official Boxing Club at Nazarbayev University is a high-energy community that focuses on discipline, physical conditioning, and the mastery of combat sports. It operates under the "Sport and Dance" category of university organizations.
The club’s core activities and goals include:
Skill Development: Regular training sessions cover the fundamentals of boxing, including footwork, defensive techniques, and striking precision. It is open to all skill levels, from complete beginners to experienced fighters.
Physical & Mental Grit: Beyond the physical workout, the club emphasizes building "warrior spirit"—resilience, self-control, and the mental toughness required to stay focused under pressure.
Intramural Competition: The club provides a pathway for students to participate in internal tournaments and university leagues, allowing members to test their skills in a controlled, competitive environment.
Structured Environment: Practices typically take place at the NU Sports Center (often in specialized halls like the Gymnastics or Combat halls), utilizing professional-grade equipment and guided by experienced peer leaders or coaches.

name: Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu and Grappling Club
description:
NU BJJ & Grappling club was established in the Spring of 2022, and has since become a major martial arts sports club at Nazarbayev University. The club fosters a strong community of health-conscious and driven athletes who push each other to excel. The students have been trained by the high-ranking coaches (Matthew Courney, Michael Aristidou, Ludy Aristidou, and Nazgul Rakhayeva).  
          The head coach of the club, Matthew Courtney, is a black belt in BJJ and Mentor of the Year 2025 by NU Awards. Under his guidance, numerous members have become prize winners and champions in the City and Republican competitions, including AIGA, AJP, GMT tournaments, and the Eurasian Jiu-Jitsu Cup. Beyond competition, the club holds self-defense workshops, advanced training masterclasses, internal tournaments, and external collaborations, enriching the student experience and promoting a culture of excellence both on and off the mat.

name: Case Club
description:
NU Case Club is the premier professional organization at Nazarbayev University dedicated to mastering the "case method"—a problem-solving framework used by top-tier consulting firms, multinational corporations, and business schools worldwide.
The club prepares students for high-pressure corporate environments through four strategic pillars:
Case Cracking & Methodology: Training members to decompose complex business problems using logical frameworks (e.g., MECE, SWOT, Porter’s Five Forces). It transforms raw data into actionable business strategies.
Case Championships: Organizing and preparing teams for prestigious national and international competitions (such as the McKinsey Business Diving or HSBC Case Challenge), where students solve real-life business dilemmas under tight deadlines.
Soft Skills & Presentation: Heavy emphasis on "storylining"—the art of creating persuasive slide decks and delivering professional pitches to a panel of "judges" or "clients."
Industry Networking: Serving as a bridge to the "Big Three" (MBB) and "Big Four" firms, hosting mock interviews, networking sessions, and workshops led by industry professionals.

name: Children’s Development Fund
description:
Children's Development Foundation (СDF) is a non-profit charity organization aiming to help children from socially vulnerable families prioritizing thoughtful and effective charity, which has a long-term positive influence. We believe that real change comes from compassion paired with action. That’s why we lead a variety of projects - both on and off campus - focused on providing opportunities, educational support, and essential resources to those who need them most. By joining our team you will gain lots of skills: hard and soft ones, become a part of our family and help those in need by providing opportunities. Don't let your heart get cold with CDF!

- Mentoring program
- Garage/Charity Sales
- Charity Trail Run

- Warm Winter project provided winter clothing to 213 children
- 51 SVC students joined the Mentoring Program
- Collaboration with 100+ partners/sponsors and 60 student clubs

- No experience needed
- Open to learning
- Commitment & Responsibility

name: Contemporary Dance
description:
Contemporary dance blends various dance styles including modern, jazz, and ballet techniques to express emotion and tell stories through movement. NU Contemporary Dance Club is a cultural entity, existing in NU since 2011. It’s a creative space for students to dance, learn new techniques and get used to stage performances.
The club members actively participate in different university events and contribute to the cultural life of the university. Our club welcomes all members and is willing to help students to create precious memories through university life. It's not just a club - it's a family.


**Location:** 26 Block 103 Room, Dormitory


## Activities
- Trainings three times a week (dance skills, stunts, acrobatics, stretching)
- Organization of Grand Concert every year (”Avatar: The last Airbender” theme - 2023, “Hunger Games” theme - 2024, “Alice in Wonderland” theme - 2025)
- Dance Workshops led by professional dancers
- Artistic photoshoots & Engaging and Creative Teambuildings


## Achievements
- Performance as a PRO-nominee at one of the biggest dance contest in Kazakhstan “Choreo 2025”
- Organization of 3 Grand Concerts that attracted a total of 2,000+ people
- TOP 3 Dance Clubs of the 2025 by NU Awards Report
- More than 60,000 views in total of the performance videos on YouTube


## Entry Requirements
- No skills are required, but there will be auditions.

name: E.Quality
description:
E.Quality at Nazarbayev University is a social awareness and advocacy organization dedicated to promoting the values of equality, inclusivity, and social justice within the campus community.
The club serves as a platform for dialogue and activism through several core functions:
Awareness & Education: Organizing workshops, guest lectures, and seminars that address topics such as gender equality, human rights, and the elimination of social stereotypes.
Safe Space Creation: Providing a supportive environment where students can openly discuss sensitive social issues and personal experiences without judgment.
Advocacy: Working to identify and address systemic challenges within the university environment to ensure all students, regardless of background, feel represented and respected.
Community Engagement: Running media campaigns and collaborative projects that encourage empathy, ethical communication, and the protection of vulnerable groups.
In short, E.Quality is the "social conscience" of the student body, striving to transform the university into a more conscious, tolerant, and inclusive community.

name: Fencing Club
description:
NU Fencing Club was founded in 2015 by a professional fencer and several professors in our university, and rebranded after quarantine by current members. Throughout this journey, members from NU Fencing has attended many tournaments and all returned with honors.
During 2024-2025 academic year, NU Fencing Club attended tournaments in city, national, and even international level, and won many places representing the club.
Join us to train, compete, and grow together!


**Location:** Sport Center, 2nd Floor, Fencing Hall


## Activities
- We hold weekly épée trainings every Tuesday and Thursday from 18:00 to 20:00, and Saturday from 12:00 to 14:00.
- Beyond fencing, our club runs through the dedication of student-led departments: Tournaments, PR/HR, External Affairs, Treasury, Events, Training, and Armoury - giving every member a chance to contribute, lead, and grow.


## Achievements
- Platinium International Fencing Cup, Female Individual, Bronze: Madina Yernur; Female Team, Silver: Madina Yernur, Fariza Idrissova
- Platinium International Fencing Cup, Male 5th place: Aldiyar Bolatov, Female 6th place: Aiganym Shaken
- For bianual Astana City Tournament organized by Diestra Fencing, 6 bronze medal (Timur Ni, Nurassyl Assylkhanuly, Gulnaz Ingkar, Madina Yernur, Kamila Orazbek, Ratmir Kanatov), 3 Silver (Aigerim Tulegenova twice, Aldiyar Baimukhametov), and 1 Gold won by Danial Akhat
- Republican Championship in Astana 2025: Danial Akhat and Alua Dosbergenova TOP 30


## Entry Requirements
- We open recruitment once per semester, which includes an application, interview, and tryouts.
- Selection is based on overall performance and fit.
- We look for members who are physically prepared, disciplined, and committed to attending trainings and contributing to club work.

name: French Club
description:
The Official French Club at Nazarbayev University is a cultural and linguistic community dedicated to promoting the French language and the diverse cultures of the Francophone world.
The club provides an immersive environment through several key activities:
Language Practice: Hosting "French Speaking Clubs" and peer-led tutoring sessions where students of all levels—from absolute beginners to advanced speakers—can practice conversational skills in an informal setting.
Cultural Immersion: Organizing events centered around French cinema, gastronomy, music, and literature. This includes celebrating international events like Fête de la Francophonie.
Networking and Opportunities: Serving as a bridge to French-speaking resources, such as the Alliance Française, and providing information about exchange programs or internships in Francophone countries.
Social Connection: Building a "petit coin de France" (a small corner of France) on campus where students with a shared passion for European culture can connect and socialize.

name: Green Society
description:
Green Society at Nazarbayev University is the leading student-driven movement dedicated to environmental sustainability and fostering an "eco-conscious" mindset both on campus and within the wider community.
The organization translates environmental theory into tangible action through several key pillars:
Eco-Infrastructure & Advocacy: Initiating and maintaining recycling programs, promoting waste segregation, and advocating for sustainable resource management within university facilities.
Education & Awareness: Hosting eco-festivals, "upcycling" workshops, and seminars on global climate change, circular economies, and conscious consumerism.
Hands-on Projects: Organizing practical outdoor activities such as tree planting, campus greening initiatives, and "plogging" (picking up litter while jogging) events.
Collaborative Impact: Partnering with environmental NGOs and sustainable brands to integrate students into national ecological projects and professional environmental networks.
In essence, Green Society provides a platform for students to transform environmental concerns into practical solutions, making the university experience more sustainable and ethically responsible.

name: iGem Club
description:
iGEM NU Kazakhstan is a multidisciplinary student team that represents Kazakhstan at the international iGEM competition. Bringing together students from biology, medicine, engineering, AI, and design, the team develops synthetic biology projects tackling real-world challenges in health, diagnostics, and the environment. Beyond research, iGEM NU runs STEM outreach programs, summer camps, biohackathons, and awareness campaigns to inspire future researchers and promote biomedical innovation in Kazakhstan.


**Location:** 27 Block 144 Room, Dormitory


## Activities
- Designing synthetic biology projects for iGEM competition
- Conducting lab-based research and data analysis
- Organizing national STEM outreach events and biohackathons
- Hosting awareness and charity campaigns
- Running workshops, summer camps, and public lectures on biotechnology
- Collaborating with scientists, companies, and universities worldwide


## Achievements
- 🥇2017, 2018, 2021 (Top-10), 2022, 2024 (Top-5 Oncology); 🥈2015, 2023; 🥉2014, 2019 at the iGEM Grand Jamboree
- Finalists at ABC Incubation X TCA 8.0 (Top 10 Teams)
- ABC Incubation XIV (Pitch Guru winner, Top 3)


## Entry Requirements
- Interest in biology, medicine, or engineering
- Willingness to collaborate across disciplines
- Commitment to research and outreach

name: Kazakh Stage
description:
Kazakh Stage is more than just a student theater club — it is a movement to revive and celebrate the Kazakh language and culture through the power of performing arts. As the only student organization at the university dedicated to theatrical performances in Kazakh, we proudly write, produce, promote, and perform original and adapted plays that speak to both tradition and modernity. From timeless Kazakh classics to creative reinterpretations of foreign literature, every piece is performed with heartfelt artistry and deep cultural meaning.

Our mission is clear: to make the Kazakh language visible, vibrant, and alive — not only on stage, but across the country and online platforms. Our entire process is in Kazakh: from scriptwriting and rehearsals to costume design, props, and event promotion. We are a self-sufficient and passionate collective of students who believe in the beauty of our heritage and express it through creativity.

Our standout performances such as “Махаббат қызық мол жылдар”, “Кішкентай ханымдар” ("Little Women"), "Қыз Жібек" and many more have touched hearts, while immersive cultural events like Cozy Korea, QazTrip, and Soul Kesh have created spaces of belonging and cultural pride for students of all backgrounds. Kazakh Stage is a big, family-like community that welcomes anyone who is passionate about the Kazakh culture.


**Location:** 26 Block 103 Room, Dormitory


## Activities
- Writing and performing original and adapted plays in Kazakh
- Costume design, props, lighting, and sound management
- Cultural events: Cozy Korea, QazTrip, Soul Kesh
- Online content production in Kazakh (storytelling, humor, promos)


## Achievements
- Meruyert Utekeshova, the actress of 'Qyz Jibek', attended our play
- Performance of 'Махаббат қызық мол жылдар' on ENU stage
- Participation in a cultural trip to Zhidebay, Semey (Abai’s homeland)
- Featured on Kazakh national TV and radio
- Performed to full audiences in Main Hall and Orange Hall


## Entry Requirements
- No strict requirements - passion, potential, and love for Kazakh culture and art
- 5 departments to join: Finance, HR, Marketing, Technical, Organizing
- Acting is optional - everyone has the chance to try

name: Kiye Qazaq Dance Club
description:
The Official Kiye Qazaq Dance Club at Nazarbayev University is a premier cultural organization dedicated to the preservation, promotion, and modern interpretation of Kazakh national dance.
The club serves as a vital guardian of heritage through several key activities:
Artistic Preservation: Mastering traditional Kazakh dances such as Kara Zhorga, Kamazhai, and various folk choreographies, ensuring that the elegance and storytelling of national art are passed down to the student body.
Performance Excellence: Representing the university at major cultural events, including the NU Global Forum, Nauryz celebrations, and official university concerts, often performing alongside the NU Orchestra.
Cultural Identity: Fostering a deep sense of national pride among students by exploring the historical context, traditional costumes, and musical foundations of Kazakh dance.
Inclusivity and Training: Providing a space for students of all backgrounds to learn national choreography, regardless of prior dance experience, thereby strengthening the multicultural dialogue within the campus.
In essence, Kiye Qazaq Dance Club is more than a performance group; it is a "living archive" that keeps Kazakh traditions vibrant and relevant in a modern academic environment.

name: Latino Social Dance Club
description:
The Latino Social Dance Club at Nazarbayev University is a vibrant community dedicated to the art of social dancing, focusing on the infectious rhythms and techniques of Latin American dance styles.
The club promotes a culture of movement and connection through several key activities:
Diverse Dance Styles: Offering workshops and training sessions in popular Latin genres such as Salsa, Bachata, Kizomba, and Merengue, catering to everyone from absolute beginners to advanced dancers.
Social Connection: Emphasizing the "social" aspect of dance, the club hosts dance parties and "socials" where students can practice their skills, meet new people, and build confidence in a relaxed, non-competitive environment.
Physical & Mental Well-being: Providing a high-energy outlet for stress relief, helping students maintain physical fitness and mental health through rhythmic exercise and creative expression.
Cultural Appreciation: Introducing the university community to the history and etiquette of Latin dance culture, fostering a sense of global awareness and rhythmic musicality.
In essence, the Latino Social Dance Club is the university’s primary hub for interpersonal connection through movement, offering a space where students can "dance their stress away" while mastering a universal social skill.

name: Legal Literacy Club
description:
Legal Literacy Club is deeply devoted to fostering legal awareness and education. We believe our mission is to empower students and community members with the knowledge of their rights, civic duties, and legal procedures through expert talks, mock trials, and interactive workshops. We explore diverse topics – from constitutional rights to social justice and digital rights – equipping the community with the knowledge to make informed decisions and seek justice. By collaborating with legal experts and academic institutions, we believe we are on a path to a more legally literate and just society.

Whether you're a law enthusiast or simply curious about your rights, join us to learn, engage, and make a difference!


**Location:** 22 Block 124 Room, Dormitory


## Activities
- Law Summer School & Workshops
- LegalTea: collaborations with legal experts
- Mock trials and debate competitions
- Anti-Harassment campaign


## Achievements
- Organised an International Law Summer School, certifying 10+ participants
- Launched an Anti-Harassment Campaign in collaboration with the Anti-Harassment Committee
- Hosted a Moot Court Competition, fostering advocacy and litigation skills
- Conducted #LegalTea Talks on contemporary legal issues with experts


## Entry Requirements
- Strong passion for legal education, social justice, and leadership
- Board application: fill out a form and attend an interview
- Moot Court application: submit a legal memorial and attend an interview
- Motivation to engage in legal advocacy and community initiatives

name: Nomad Debate Club
description:
Founded in 2011 as a debate club, we welcome everyone who is not afraid to learn debating. Our members achieved success not only in Central Asian tournaments, but also in world-class championships, our alumni are top students and experts in their field across the world. Interested? All you have to do is to show up on our weekly meetings every Tuesday and Thursday where we hold rounds or lectures with workshops.

We aim to share vibrant experiences and actively interact with other large debate communities and student clubs as well. And most importantly, Nomad is not about debates, it's about community. With our members you can engage in lively discussions on diverse topics, in our club you will build confidence and teamwork, and with the help of our community you will become a more curious and motivated debater.


**Location:** Block 8

- Exclusive Mentorship Programs with Lectures and Workshops

- Best Judges of Central Asian and Kazakhstan Debate Championships

name: NU Basketball
description:
The Official Basketball Club at Nazarbayev University is one of the most popular and competitive sports organizations on campus, catering to both serious athletes and casual players. It serves as the primary hub for anyone looking to develop their skills on the court.
The club’s activity is structured around three main objectives:
Varsity Excellence: The club oversees the selection and training of the university's representative teams (Men’s and Women’s), which compete in the National Student League and various inter-university tournaments across Kazakhstan.
Intramural Community: For students not on the varsity roster, the club organizes internal leagues, 3x3 tournaments, and "Open Gym" sessions. This allows the broader student body to engage in the sport regardless of their skill level.
Fundamental Training: Regular practice sessions focus on technical proficiency, including shooting mechanics, defensive positioning, and tactical team plays, often guided by experienced coaches or senior student-athletes.
Culture of Discipline: The club fosters a high-intensity environment that mirrors professional sports, emphasizing teamwork, physical endurance, and the "Mamba Mentality" of constant self-improvement.

name: NU Choir
description:
We are NU Choir - a performing arts club that’s been inspiring audiences for over 13 years, not just at concerts, but in everything we do! Our goal isn’t just to organise cool events, but also to help you grow: as a singer, as a leader, and as a person. It’s about finding what you truly love, meeting like-minded people, and exploring yourself along the way. At our concerts, we manifest our ideas and messages through beautifully harmonised songs that tell stories and evoke a wide range of emotions. Our repertoire is as diverse as our members: Rock, Pop, Jazz, Disney classics, spooky Halloween-ish songs, childhood favorites, in English, Kazakh, in some other language, even some songs with gibberish lyrics! There are NO limits! It’s not always easy, but we believe the challenge will help you discover what truly brings you joy. Can't wait to see you at our audition!


**Location:** Cinema Hall, 25 Block k021


## Activities
- Themed Concerts
- Creative Events
- Team-Buildings and Hang-outs


## Achievements
- Performed at OYU Festival 2024
- Performed with Moldanazar on Orynkhan's Concert
- Mega Silk Way Concerts
- Annual Heart Winners on Singing Valentines


## Entry Requirements
- Passion for singing and/or organizing events
- Desire to learn and be a part of a big community
- Some ear training (nothing fancy)
- Any additional skills are welcome!

name: NU Climbing
description:
The Official Climbing Club at Nazarbayev University is a specialized community focused on the sport of rock climbing and bouldering, emphasizing physical strength, problem-solving, and safety.
The club provides a unique athletic experience through several core pillars:
Technical Mastery: Training focuses on the fundamentals of bouldering and top-rope climbing, including proper footwork, grip techniques (crimps, jugs, slopers), and body positioning to navigate "problems" on the wall.
Safety & Gear: A heavy emphasis is placed on safety protocols, including the correct use of harnesses, climbing shoes, chalk, and belay techniques, ensuring a secure environment for both beginners and veterans.
Mental Strategy: Climbing at NU is often described as "physical chess." Members learn to analyze routes and plan their movements ahead of time, fostering critical thinking and mental focus under physical exertion.
Community & Outdoor Trips: Beyond the campus climbing wall, the club fosters a tight-knit community that often organizes outdoor excursions to natural climbing spots in Kazakhstan, bridging the gap between indoor training and real-world rock climbing.
In essence, NU Climbing is for students who seek a non-traditional workout that challenges both their muscles and their minds in a supportive, vertical environment.

name: NU Comedy Club
description:
NU Comedy Club is a stand-up collective founded by Nazarbayev University alumni and creators of the SU!N Project - Alexander Merkul (SEDS-CS), Aman Tuyluygenov (SSH-economics), and Alikhan Karymsakov (NUSOM). Many of our alumni are now popular comedians, which include Arman Qonkobay, Abdybek Urmanov, Yaroslav Mironov, Alisher Toregali, Ayana Mussabayeva, Miras Samat, Abylay Dossmurzayev, Yergali Temirgali.
Equally central to our identity is social responsibility. Since the early days of our performances, we’ve collaborated closely with NU Red Crescent, organizing shows where proceeds or donations go toward charitable causes. Through these events, we aim not only to make people laugh but also to support our community and those in need.


**Location:** 27 Block 147 Room, Dormitory


## Activities
- Stand up concerts
- Open mics
- Lookalike competitions
- Roast battles


## Achievements
- NU Awards Small Scale Event of the Year nominees 2024-2025


## Entry Requirements
- Everyone is welcome

name: NU Decides
description:
NU Decides is the official interschool song contest of Nazarbayev University, where six schools compete for the title of Best Singer School. What makes this event unique is its transparency and spirit of student involvement - it is YOU who decide the winner.
From auditions to the Grand Finale, the NU Decides Organizing Committee handles every step: stage design, artist support, promotion, logistics, and live voting through a custom-built digital platform. It has brought together over 20 student clubs, involved students from other universities, and reached thousands of viewers both on campus and online.
For the past two years, the Grand Finale has been proudly held in collaboration with the Swedish Embassy in Kazakhstan, whose support has helped to increase the scale and cultural reach of the event. Whether you’re on stage, behind the scenes, or cheering from the audience - NU Decides is where voices are heard, stories are shared, and memories are made.


**Location:** 27 Block 146 Room, Dormitory (Spring '25)


## Activities
- Nu Decides Grand Final 2025
- Nu Decides Grand Final 2024
- Nu Decides Grand Final 2023
- Nu Decides Grand Final 2022


## Achievements
- Established an ongoing partnership with the Embassy of Sweden.
- Organized Grand Finales in 2022, 2023, 2024, and 2025, attracting a combined audience of over 4,000+ attendees, including students, faculty, and university guests.
- Collaborated with over 20 student clubs across NU and engaged students from other universities in both stage performance and media production.
- Consistently produce professional video content, utilizing advanced camera equipment, lighting setups, and sound engineers to ensure premium-quality stage production.


## Entry Requirements
- Genuine interest in working with participants, media production, music, and concert management.
- Strong sense of responsibility, teamwork, and time management.
- Willingness to contribute actively and take initiative.
- Prior experience in student organizations or event planning is a plus, but not required.

name: NU Esports
description:
The Official Esports Club at Nazarbayev University is a dynamic community dedicated to competitive gaming, digital entertainment, and the professionalization of the gaming industry within the university.
The club operates as a hub for both elite competitors and casual enthusiasts through several key pillars:
Competitive Excellence: Managing and training the university’s varsity rosters for popular titles such as Dota 2, Counter-Strike, League of Legends, and Valorant. These teams represent NU in national and international student leagues.
Tournament Organization: Hosting large-scale campus events, including "Cyber-Athletics" tournaments and viewing parties for major global championships (The International, Worlds), fostering a strong "spectator" culture.
Skill & Strategy: Beyond just playing, the club emphasizes meta-analysis, tactical coordination, and team dynamics, helping players move from "casual gaming" to a structured, professional mindset.
Gaming Infrastructure: Facilitating access to gaming resources and creating a networking space for students interested in game streaming, shoutcasting, and the technical side of esports production.

name: NU GDC
description:
The Game Development Club (GameDev NU) at Nazarbayev University is a multidisciplinary community that brings together programmers, artists, designers, and storytellers to create interactive digital experiences from scratch.
The club serves as an incubator for creative technology through several key pillars:
Multidisciplinary Collaboration: Acting as a meeting point for Computer Science students (mechanics and physics), CG Club members (visual assets), and writers (narrative design) to form cohesive development teams.
Engine Mastery: Providing workshops and resources for learning industry-standard game engines such as Unity (C#) and Unreal Engine (C++/Blueprints), as well as lightweight frameworks for 2D games and game jams.
Game Jams: Organizing and participating in intensive "marathon" coding events where teams must build a functional game prototype within 48 to 72 hours based on a specific theme.
The Full Pipeline: Educating members on the entire lifecycle of a game, from initial concept and "gray-boxing" to playtesting, bug fixing, and final deployment on platforms like itch.io.

name: NU IMechE SC
description:
The NU IMechE Student Chapter is a student-led organization dedicated to supporting engineering students in initiating and advancing their careers in the engineering field. Our mission is to bridge the gap between industry, academia, and research through initiatives like the Mechaton industrial case championship, guest lectures by industry professionals and experienced students, and field trips to leading industrial plants. The Chapter facilitates access to internship opportunities with leading industrial companies such as Wabtec Corporation, Kazakhstan Paramount Engineering, and Airbus Helicopters Kazakhstan Engineering, primarily through the Mechaton case championship. In addition, NU IMechE SC organizes familiarization tours to major industrial enterprises in Kazakhstan, including the facilities of Kazakhstan Paramount Engineering, Alstom, Wabtec Corporation, and Airbus Helicopters Kazakhstan Engineering, enabling students to gain first-hand insights into real-world working environments. The Chapter also hosts a variety of smaller-scale events aimed at career development. These include guest sessions with students who have successfully completed internships at prominent technology firms - such as Mercedes-Benz and Wabtec - who share their experiences and provide guidance on internship application processes. Furthermore, professionals from both industry and academia are regularly invited to speak on topics relevant to their areas of expertise, offering students diverse perspectives on engineering careers. Each year, NU IMechE SC also organizes MechParty, a team-building event for students and faculty members of the Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering Department. This event serves to strengthen the relationship between students and academic staff through informal interaction, while also providing an opportunity for students across various cohorts and specializations within mechanical engineering to connect and collaborate.


**Location:** 27 Block 145 Room, Dormitory


## Activities
- Mechaton
- Mechparty
- Field Trips to KPE, Alstom & Airbus helicopters


## Achievements
- We have good connections with the industry (Alstom, Airbus Helicopters Kazakhstan Engineering, Wabtec Corporation and Kazakhstan Paramount Engineering). Through our Mechathon event, 5 people received paid internships at Wabtec, 5 people received unpaid internships at Airbus Helicopters Kazakhstan Engineering, and 4 people received unpaid internships at Kazakhstan Paramount Engineering


## Entry Requirements
- Desire to work on events and work related to the topic of community development for engineers.
- Ability to work in PR, HR, SMM, treasurer, and event organization.
- Ability to work in canvas and photo, and video editing is welcome.
- The main requirement is the desire to work for the good of the club.

These include guest sessions with students who have successfully completed internships at prominent technology firms - such as Mercedes-Benz and Wabtec - who share their experiences and provide guidance on internship application processes. Furthermore, professionals from both industry and academia are regularly invited to speak on topics relevant to their areas of expertise, offering students diverse perspectives on engineering careers. Each year, NU IMechE SC also organizes MechParty, a team-building event for students and faculty members of the Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering Department. This event serves to strengthen the relationship between students and academic staff through informal interaction, while also providing an opportunity for students across various cohorts and specializations within mechanical engineering to connect and collaborate. Activities Mechaton Mechparty Field Trips to KPE, Alstom & Airbus helicopters Achievements We have good connections with the industry (Alstom, Airbus Helicopters Kazakhstan Engineering, Wabtec Corporation and Kazakhstan Paramount Engineering). Through our Mechathon event, 5 people received paid internships at Wabtec, 5 people received unpaid internships at Airbus Helicopters Kazakhstan Engineering, and 4 people received unpaid internships at Kazakhstan Paramount Engineering Entry Requirements Desire to work on events and work related to the topic of community development for engineers. Ability to work in PR, HR, SMM, treasurer, and event organization. Ability to work in canvas and photo, and video editing is welcome. The main requirement is the desire to work for the good of the club.

name: NU Korean Club
description:
NU Korean Club is an academic and cultural club that focuses on popularizing Korean culture among students. We organise educational sessions, language courses, webinars, and other theme events.The Official Korean Club at Nazarbayev University is a vibrant cultural organization dedicated to sharing the rich traditions, modern trends, and linguistic heritage of South Korea with the university community.
The club provides an immersive experience through several core pillars:
Language & Education: Hosting "Korean Speaking Clubs" and informal tutoring sessions for students interested in learning Hangul and improving their conversational skills, often in collaboration with native speakers or advanced learners.
K-Pop & Entertainment: Serving as a major hub for fans of Korean music and dramas. The club often collaborates with dance groups like YBS for performances and hosts "K-Pop random dance" events and drama screenings.
Cultural Traditions: Organizing workshops on traditional aspects of Korean life, including Hanbok (traditional clothing) showcases, calligraphy, and celebrations of holidays like Chuseok or Seollal.
Culinary Experiences: Hosting food festivals and cooking workshops where students can learn about and taste popular Korean dishes like Tteokbokki, Kimchi, and Kimbap

name: NU MUN
description:
Nazarbayev University Model United Nations (NU MUN) is a premier academic organization that simulates the inner workings of the United Nations, providing a platform for students to engage in high-level diplomacy and international relations.
The club focuses on developing the leaders of tomorrow through several core activities:
Diplomatic Simulations: Members take on the roles of delegates from different countries, debating urgent global issues—ranging from international security to climate policy—within various UN committees (e.g., DISEC, SOCHUM, Security Council).
Skill Development: Heavy emphasis is placed on honing public speaking, professional negotiation, and formal resolution writing. Members learn the "Rules of Procedure" used in international governance.
Annual Conference: The club organizes the NU MUN Conference, a flagship event that attracts students from across Kazakhstan and beyond, fostering a competitive yet collaborative environment for solving world crises.
Critical Thinking & Research: Delegates must conduct deep dives into the foreign policies of their assigned nations, requiring a high degree of analytical rigor and the ability to view global events from multiple perspectives.

Founded in 2015 and with a deep history, the NU Orchestra brings together national and contemporary art, while delighting the NU community with one of its largest concerts of the year at the University.

name: NU Rover
description:
NU Rover is an ambitious student engineering team dedicated to developing space exploration rovers and drones for international robotics engineering competitions across the world, including in Canada, Australia, the United States, and more. We bring together students from various disciplines – mechanics, electronics, control, automatization, astrobiology – to design and build our rover and drone for solving real-world space exploration challenges, such as autonomous navigation, equipment servicing, in-situ chemical analysis of Martian soil, and more.

Visit our website aspanrover.kz to learn more about our projects, explore open roles, and apply. We offer rolling admissions – so anyone can apply to join our team at any time.


**Location:** 27 Block 144 Room, Dormitory; Lab: S4.304


## Activities
- Designing and manufacturing Mars rovers for international competitions
- Developing autonomous navigation and equipment servicing systems
- Conducting in-situ chemical analysis of Martian soil
- Building drones for space exploration challenges


## Achievements
- First team from Kazakhstan, Central Asia, and Former USSR to qualify for Canadian International Rover Challenge (2025), Australian Rover Challenge (2025)
- Top-20 team at the Australian Rover Challenge 2025
- Top-20 team at the Canadian International Rover Challenge 2025
- Top-30% team at the European Rover Challenge 2025
- Perfect science score (16/16) at the University Rover Challenge 2025


## Entry Requirements
- Technical experience
- Strong motivation & Willigness to learn
- Teamwork mindset
- Learn more about requirements for specific roles at aspanrover.kz/careers

name: NU Runners
description:
The NU Runners club is the primary community at Nazarbayev University for distance running, endurance training, and outdoor fitness. It caters to a wide spectrum of athletes, from those training for their first 5K to seasoned marathon runners.
The club promotes a lifestyle of consistency and health through several core activities:
Group Runs: Organizing scheduled runs around the campus perimeter, the linear park, or the Astana Botanical Garden. These sessions provide a social motivation that helps students maintain a regular training habit.
Race Preparation: Helping members prepare for local and national events, such as the Astana Marathon or seasonal charity runs, by providing structured training plans and pacing groups.
Fitness Education: Sharing knowledge on proper running mechanics, injury prevention (warm-ups and stretching), and cardiovascular health to ensure members train safely and effectively.
Community Spirit: Fostering a "leave no one behind" culture where runners of all paces—from "joggers" to "sprinters"—support each other, making the grueling nature of endurance training more enjoyable.

name: NU SciPod
description:
NU SciPod is the podcast club, created to make science accessible to a wider audience. Our podcasts feature professors and researchers who share their experience in biology, chemistry, medicine, and beyond. At the same time, we invite students to share their perspectives and research journeys, creating discussions that connect science with everyday university life.


## Activities
- Podcast preparation - from scriptwriting to equipment set up
- Podcast production - filming, editing, and publishing
- Networking with top professors, doctors, and researchers
- Organizing podcast-themed university events and collaborations


## Achievements
- Produced 11 podcasts featuring top specialists, including NU’s Dean of SSH
- Built strong collaborations with researchers across multiple disciplines
- Expanded NU’s research visibility by promoting podcasts to wider audiences


## Entry Requirements
- Open to all backgrounds
- Eager to share science in an easy way
- Ready to learn and create
- Commitment to teamwork and shared values

name: NU Shyraq
description:
Shyraq Club is Nazarbayev University's official Kazakh language and culture club. Our mission is to preserve, celebrate, and promote the rich heritage of the Kazakh people among students from all backgrounds. The name "Shyraq" symbolizes the light of our traditions – something we carry forward with pride and purpose.

Through cultural events, language workshops, national holidays, storytelling sessions, film screenings, and creative performances, we create a space where students can reconnect with or learn about Kazakh history, customs, literature, and identity.

Whether you're a native speaker or just beginning to explore Kazakh culture, Shyraq Club is a welcoming community that encourages curiosity, expression, and national pride.

Come join us to light the torch of heritage - and pass it on to future generations.


**Location:** 26 Block 128 Room, Dormitory


## Activities
- Oqylym-Aitylym sessions
- Kazakh Toastmasters sessions
- Kazakh language workshops (beginner to advanced)
- Nauryz and national holiday celebrations


## Achievements
- Founded one of the first student-led Kazakh Toastmasters initiatives at NU, empowering students to speak confidently in Kazakh
- Conducted beginner Kazakh courses for NU community


## Entry Requirements
- Basic proficiency in the Kazakh language
- Hospitality and a friendly attitude
- A genuine interest in Kazakh language, traditions, and national identity

name: NU Student Investment Fund
description:
The Nazarbayev University Student Investment Fund (NUSIF) is an elite, student-led organization that functions as a real-world investment firm, allowing members to manage a virtual (and sometimes real-asset-backed) portfolio while mastering the intricacies of the financial markets.
The fund operates through a professional corporate structure to achieve several objectives:
Equity Research & Analysis: Members are divided into sector teams (e.g., Tech, Energy, Healthcare) where they perform "bottom-up" fundamental analysis, build financial models, and pitch stocks to the investment committee.
Portfolio Management: Developing and executing investment strategies, managing risk, and understanding asset allocation. This involves tracking market trends, macroeconomic indicators, and corporate earnings.
Professional Networking: Serving as a bridge to the finance industry, often hosting guest speakers from the Astana International Financial Centre (AIFC), tier-1 investment banks, and Big Four accounting firms.
Technical Literacy: Training members in the use of professional tools like Bloomberg Terminals, Excel for financial modeling, and Python for quantitative analysis or algorithmic trading.

- Research & evidence-based medicine events
- Career development sessions

## Entry Requirements
- Open to all students
- Active participation
- Interest in clinical skills and medicine
- Commitment to club values

- Field Trips

- Nominated for "Social Contribution of the Year 2024"

name: NU Theater
description:
The goal of the club is to help people learn to express their emotions freely, gain confidence, perform on stage, write and direct plays, and develop their acting skills. NU Theater is a club with around 80 members that conducts rehearsals twice a week in the Cinema Hall. The club regularly hosts a minimum of 2 events each year: Miriater (small-scale event for the new members, one per semester) and one big-scale event. Our Club wants students of NU to learn more about the beauty of theater. We try to allow students to try being actors and directors. Each semester, we make big performances based on original and adapted scripts, showcasing the talents of our members.


**Location:** 26 Block 103 Room, Dormitory / Cinema Hall


## Activities
- Workshops for new members & 2 rehearsals every week
- Departments for various talents to contribute to performances
- Visits to mentor's studio 'Reka' for artistic expression
- Team-buildings and ice-breaking activities like game nights and movie viewings


## Achievements
- Revived tradition 'Miriater' allowing both new and older members to perform, write, and direct
- Performed for WHO's Sub-mental Health Forum
- First-ever two-day performance of 'Тень' in Orange Hall, 700 registered guests, nominated for Small-Scale Event of the Year
- Large-Scale Event of the Year 'Dead Poets Society' with an audience of 900 people


## Entry Requirements
- At the beginning of each semester through recruitment process
- Follow our social media channels for the latest updates

description:

Whether you’re here to grow as a player or simply be part of something meaningful - you’re welcome with us.


**Location:** Sport & Athletic Center

- Female students only
- No skill level required — beginners are welcome
- Regular participation in training sessions

name: NU_Yoga Stretch Pilates_CLUB
description:
NU_Yoga Stretch Pilates_CLUB is a student organization that helps students improve their physical and mental well-being, reduce stress, and deepen their awareness through holistic practices. The club offers yoga, pilates, stretching, meditation sessions, and workshops for all skill levels, from beginners to advanced practitioners. With a focus on strength, flexibility, posture, balance, and inner calm, the club fosters a supportive and non-competitive community where members can build resilience, cultivate mindfulness, and promote overall wellness.


**Location:** Nazarbayev University


## Activities
- Meditation – guided sessions promoting calmness, inner peace, and relaxation
- Ashtanga Yoga – dynamic practice synchronizing breath with physically demanding postures
- Hatha Yoga – balancing the body and mind through postures and breathwork
- Vinyasa – flowing sequences that link breath to movement seamlessly
- Yoga Flow – continuous, fluid yoga practice synchronized with breath
- Pilates Matwork – improving core strength, posture, and flexibility
- Somatic Pilates – combining mindful body awareness with strength and control
- Stretching – exercises to enhance flexibility and muscle tone
- Port de Bras – low-impact conditioning workout for posture and muscle balance
- Pilates Power – high-intensity variation of traditional Pilates
- Healthy Back – strengthening exercises for spinal stability and flexibility


## Achievements
- At about 25 group programs available.
- More than 70 classes every month.
- Our classes are led by experienced and professional coaches who easily connect with each group member, regardless of their fitness level.
- Programs for all physical qualities collected in one schedule, which combine wellness, functional, circuit, strength training blocks, cardio elements, dance, stretching, yoga, pilates, soft fitness, additional bonus classes on meditation, nutrition, master classes in various areas with experts, free training, online classes, teambuildings and much more.
- Our club held many events - it were really an exciting moments. We organized events, including wellness & mind & body activities, concerts, fitness conventions, retreats, nutrition workshops. We support and participate in various charity events and stress relief days. Also we are conducting warm up workouts annual arrangements within orientation week.
- These aren't just workouts with a standard set of programs. They're a unique and special atmosphere of a sports community of like-minded people. Here, you'll immerse yourself in an atmosphere of positivity and self-improvement!


## Entry Requirements
- All levels and ages are welcome!

name: Persian Club
description:
The Official Persian Club at Nazarbayev University is a cultural and educational organization dedicated to exploring the rich history, art, and linguistic beauty of the Persian-speaking world (Iran, Tajikistan, and parts of Afghanistan).
The club fosters a deeper understanding of Persian heritage through several key pillars:
Language & Literature: Providing introductory workshops on the Persian language (Farsi) and hosting reading sessions focused on the works of legendary poets like Rumi, Khayyam, and Ferdowsi.
Cultural Celebrations: Organizing vibrant events for traditional holidays, most notably Nowruz (the Persian New Year), featuring the "Haft-sin" table and traditional music performances.
Artistic Expression: Exploring the aesthetics of Persian culture through calligraphy workshops, traditional music appreciation, and screenings of acclaimed Iranian cinema.
Historical Context: Hosting discussions on the long-standing historical and linguistic ties between Persian culture and the Central Asian region, highlighting the shared heritage found in the Silk Road history.

name: Poetry Club
description:
People come to our club for poetry - but they stay for the community.
"We don’t read and write poetry because it’s cute. We read and write poetry because we are members of the human race, and the human race is filled with passion."

- Staged and performed musicals 'Gorgorod' and 'Orpheus and Eurydice'
- Organized workshop with Arlyce Rose Menzies

- Be nice and engage in activities

name: Public Health Club
description:
The Official Public Health Club at Nazarbayev University is a student-led organization dedicated to advocating for disease prevention, health education, and the promotion of wellness within the campus and the broader community.


The club serves as a platform for proactive health advocacy through several key initiatives:


Awareness & Advocacy: Conducting information campaigns on critical public health issues, such as vaccination, nutrition, hygiene, and reproductive health, often in alignment with the UN's Sustainable Development Goal 3 (Good Health and Well-being).  
Community Outreach: Organizing health-related volunteering opportunities and outreach programs that aim to improve public well-being through practical service and education.  
Preventive Education: Hosting workshops and guest lectures that teach students and staff how to prevent chronic diseases and adopt healthier lifestyle habits.
Professional Development: Creating a network for students interested in healthcare management, epidemiology, and health policy, often collaborating with the NU School of Medicine and healthcare organizations.

name: Robotics Club
description:
The Official Robotics Club at Nazarbayev University is a premier technical organization that brings together students from engineering, computer science, and physics to design, build, and program autonomous systems.
The club serves as an interdisciplinary bridge between theory and hardware through several key activities:
Hands-on Hardware Prototyping: Members gain access to advanced tools such as 3D printers, laser cutters, and printed circuit board (PCB) mills to transform digital designs into physical machines.
Embedded Systems & Control: Workshops focus on the "brain" of the robot, teaching members how to work with microcontrollers (Arduino, STM32, Raspberry Pi), sensor integration, and PID control algorithms.
Competitive Engineering: The club often prepares teams for national and international competitions, such as Eurobot or local VEX and all-terrain challenges, where bots must autonomously navigate obstacles or complete complex tasks.
Research Integration: Many club members collaborate with the NU SEDS Robotics Department and the Institute of Smart Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ISSAI), applying machine learning and computer vision to real-world robotic solutions like automated storage systems or smart irrigation.

name: Sample Magazine
description:
Sample is a platform where art, culture, and style under students' voices collide. Each semester, we bring one magazine issue to life through original articles, eye-catching layouts, and creative photoshoots. However, we’re more than just a magazine.
We collaborate with a wide range of student clubs - from music bands to photographers and dance clubs - to show off diverse talents of NU students. We collaborate with 20+ passionate student designers, 10+ writers, and 10+ graphic designers, each bringing their own vision to the evolving world of Central Asian fashion.
Throughout the year, we host vibrant and inclusive fashion events such as thrifting parties, art performances, fashion design contests, and our highly anticipated annual runway show, which gathers over 300 attendees. Sustainability and environmental care are at the heart of our work - from promoting second-hand fashion to using eco-conscious materials in our designs.
At Sample, creativity meets community, and every issue is a celebration of student expression, creativity, and purpose.


**Location:** 27 Block 147 Room, Dormitory


## Activities
- Magazine making & Fashion Runways
- Thrifting parties & Live art performances
- Fashion design and Photography Contests & Creative Photoshoots
- Social Media Promo activity & Internal fashion-related workshops


## Achievements
- The first hard-copy magazine made by students to be stored at NU library
- Collaborations with large Kazakh media organizations - Partners Media Group, Elle Kazakhstan, Harper's Bazaar - and Central Asian influencers - @ohmybabyzee @nazira_in_zvuk


## Entry Requirements
- Have passion for creativity and fashion
- Have skills related for intended member position
- Ability to work in a team

name: SDSN Youth Kazakhstan
description:
SDSN Youth Kazakhstan at Nazarbayev University is a specialized student branch of the UN Sustainable Development Solutions Network (SDSN). It serves as a bridge between high-level global policy and grassroots student action, mobilizing youth to implement the 17 Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) within Kazakhstan.


The club operates through several strategic pillars:
Applied Awareness & Advocacy: Organizing high-impact campus events like "SDG Week," which includes interactive quizzes, movie screenings (e.g., The True Cost), and art exhibitions aimed at educating the student body on responsible consumption and social justice.  
Climate & Environment Action: Leading technical and awareness projects such as the Local Conference of Youth (LCOY) Kazakhstan and "Climate Talks," where students engage with professionals to develop local strategies for climate mitigation.  
Storytelling & Research: Managing the "Sustainability in Stories" initiative, a trilingual (Kazakh, Russian, English) publication that showcases youth-led sustainable projects and research from across the country to a global audience.  
Practical Innovation: Partnering with organizations like the British Embassy or Urban Forum Kazakhstan to host case contests and workshops on "Regenerative Culture" and "Disaster Risk Reduction," challenging students to build tangible solutions for urban and social issues

name: SEDS.KZ
description:
SEDS stands for Students for the Exploration and Development of Space. It was founded in 1980 at MIT by Peter Diamandis. Today SEDS has 85 chapters in the USA, over 30 international branches and consists of 10,000 members around the globe.

SEDS pursues to promote the exploration and development of space by educating people about the benefits of space, supporting a network of interested students, providing an opportunity for members to develop their leadership skills, and inspiring people through their involvement in space-related projects. We believe in a space-faring civilization and that focusing the enthusiasm of students is the key to humankind's future in space.


**Location:** 27 Block 144 Room, Dormitory


## Activities
- Stargazings
- Rocket Launches
- Treasure Hunts
- Space Talks
- Space Topic Related Lectures


## Achievements
- Annually conducted Cosmothon event, a space-themed hackathon
- Participation at rocketry contests and forums


## Entry Requirements
- Interest in new technologies
- Team working skills
- Wish to achieve great results

name: Sociology and Anthropology Students Association
description:
The Sociology and Anthropology Students Association (SASA) at Nazarbayev University is a specialized academic and professional organization designed for those interested in the systematic study of human behavior, social structures, and cultural diversity.
The association provides a platform for intellectual and social growth through several key pillars:
Academic Discourse & Research: Organizing "Sociology/Anthropology Talks" and seminars where students present their research or discuss contemporary social theories. The club often hosts guest speakers from the international academic community.
Fieldwork & Methodological Training: Providing workshops on qualitative and quantitative research methods—such as ethnography, participant observation, and survey design—helping students transition from classroom theory to practical data collection.
Social & Cultural Engagement: Hosting "Cinema Clubs" and discussion circles that analyze films and documentaries through a sociological lens, fostering critical thinking about power, identity, and globalization.
Professional Integration: Building a network between undergraduate students and faculty members, assisting with graduate school applications, and exploring career paths in policy, NGOs, marketing research, and academia.

name: Student Awards
description:
The Annual NU Student Awards is the university’s most prestigious event-driven organization, dedicated to recognizing and celebrating excellence within the student body, student clubs, and the broader university community.


Unlike hobby-based clubs, this organization functions as a high-stakes event production and management team, focusing on several key pillars:
Honoring Excellence: Managing the formal nomination and selection process for various awards, such as "Student of the Year," "Best Student Club," and "Contribution to University Life," ensuring transparency and merit-based recognition.
Large-Scale Event Production: Coordinating the logistics for the annual ceremony—often the biggest event in the NU calendar—including venue management, stage design, technical production, and guest relations.
Creative Content & Scripting: Developing the narrative for the ceremony through scriptwriting, video production, and performance coordination to ensure a high-quality, professional entertainment experience.
Community Integration: Acts as a unifying force on campus by bringing together athletes, artists, researchers, and student leaders to celebrate their collective achievements over the academic year.

name: Student Government
description:
Student Government NU

The Nazarbayev University Student Government (NU SG) is the supreme representative body of the student self-governance system. Modeled after the governmental structure of the Republic of Kazakhstan, it acts as the vital bridge between the student body and the university administration.
The organization operates as a "school of management" through several functional branches:
Representation & Advocacy: SG members sit on high-level University committees, including the Academic Council, ensuring student perspectives are considered in the design of academic policies, course modifications, and campus regulations.
Student Life & Social Initiatives: The SG oversees the registration and funding of student clubs, organizes large-scale traditional events (like Nauryz celebrations or the Integrity Forum), and manages social projects aimed at improving student well-being.
Professional Development: Through various ministries and committees, the SG facilitates workshops, leadership training, and networking events designed to build active citizenship and managerial skills.
External Relations: Representing the NU student community in national and international forums, collaborating with other universities and youth organizations in Kazakhstan and abroad.

name: Turkish Club
description:
The Official Turkish Club at Nazarbayev University is a cultural organization dedicated to fostering a deep appreciation for the language, history, and modern life of Turkey, while strengthening the historical and linguistic ties between the Turkish and Kazakh peoples.
The club provides a platform for cultural exchange through several key pillars:
Language & Conversation: Organizing "Turkish Speaking Clubs" for learners of all levels, helping students master Turkish grammar and vocabulary while practicing with native speakers or advanced students.
Cultural Celebrations: Hosting events centered around Turkish national holidays and traditions, such as Republic Day or the traditional Turkish Breakfast (Kahvaltı), which serves as a major social gathering for the community.
Art & Media: Exploring the global influence of Turkish cinema and television (Dizi) through screening events, and hosting workshops on traditional arts like Ebru (paper marbling) or calligraphy.
Tourism & History: Providing insights into Turkey’s diverse geography—from the historic landmarks of Istanbul to the landscapes of Cappadocia—and discussing the shared Oghuz roots that connect Turkish and Kazakh cultures.

name: Vox Club
description:
Our club avoided the COVID-19 crisis by commencing after in-person classes had been restored in Spring 2023 when Dr. Jim Hutchinson (our mentor and the very embodiment of a living mind) first proposed to conduct discussions where every student was welcome to participate. Several initial meetings took place in which different topics were discussed in a friendly and engaging manner. These meetings were gathering, on average, 10 to 20 students. On one occasion, it was suggested to bring these meetings to a higher qualitative level of existence by establishing them as a club.

Two volunteers raised their hands, Azamat Salamat and Adiya Yessengali. And thus, the official story of our Philosophy Club Vox began. If you wonder about the reason for choosing this name, the answer is: “It sounded cool and in Latin.”

It is worth mentioning that there is no Philosophy major at Nazarbayev University, and so far, only a minor in Philosophy and Religions is available. Therefore, our club unites people with sometimes surprising backgrounds, from petroleum and chemical engineering to world language and literature. This *ipso facto* creates a unique atmosphere of openness and multidimensionality, where everyone feels similarly entitled to share their ideas, and people are connected by the common love of philosophy and a strive to find the truth.


**Location:** 26 Block 129 Room, Dormitory


## Activities
- Professor-led lectures & Student-led lectures
- Weekly Philosophy Book Club meetings
- Ethics Bowl & Tealosophies
- Phil&His Connection Party


## Achievements
- Hosted Professor Russ Shafer-Landau's lecture in NU
- Recognized by American Philosophical Association


## Entry Requirements
- Keen interest in Philosophy
- Eagerness to learn something new!
